channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Irgm1 Restrains CD8+ T Cell Cytokine Production and Apoptosis via Cell-Extrinsic Regulation of Type I Interferon Signaling.
item.description: European journal of immunology - Immunity-related GTPases (IRGs) are a family of proteins that maintain cellular homeostasis by promoting autophagy and mitophagy. Mutations in human IRGM and genetic deletion of mouse Irgm1 have be...

item.title: IL-22 induces Paneth cell metaplasia in the colonic epithelium of ulcerative colitis, promoting wound healing via REG3A.
item.description: Nature communications - Paneth cell metaplasia (PCM) is a phenomenon in which Paneth cells, typically found in the small intestine, appear in the colonic epithelium of patients with ulcerative colitis (UC). Our study demo...

item.title: Modulation of mTOR, NLRP3, and Nrf2/HO-1 signaling by atorvastatin and nitazoxanide in experimental ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Naunyn-Schmiedeberg's archives of pharmacology - The exact etiology of ulcerative colitis (UC) remains incompletely understood. However, substantial evidence implicates multiple signaling pathways in its pathogenesis, including interleukin-6 (IL-...

item.title: A novel enema formulation containing cannabidiol and sodium propionate for ulcerative colitis: preclinical assessment in a murine model.
item.description: Journal of cannabis research -

item.title: Immune dysregulation, apoptosis impairment, and enhanced seroreactivity to Anisakis simplex in Crohn's disease: interplay of IL-7/IL-7R signalling and CD132 deficiencyFIRST REVIEW ROUND - REVIEWERS COMMENTSREVIEWERS COMMENTSREVIEWERS COMMENTS.
item.description: Memorias do Instituto Oswaldo Cruz - In previous studies, we identified a deficiency of γδ T cells and an increased prevalence of anti-Anisakis simplex antibodies in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). Additionally, decreased gene exp...

item.title: A Combined Colon Organoid-Sensory Neuron Model Reveals Epithelial Contribution to Moringin Efficacy Against Painful Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Phytotherapy research : PTR - Visceral pain is a major symptom of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), requiring effective treatment strategies. Gut epithelium, beyond maintaining barrier integrity and microbiota homeostasis, mo...

item.title: Biotransformation of Maclekarpine E in Rats: CYP2C19-Mediated Metabolism, Fecal Enrichment, and Network Pharmacology-Based Anti-Ulcerative Colitis Prediction.
item.description: Current issues in molecular biology - Maclekarpine E is a minor alkaloid from Macleaya species with reported in vitro anti-inflammatory activity, but its in vivo metabolism remains unexplored. This study investigated the metabolic fate...

item.title: Correction: Modulation of inflammatory and oxidative stress pathways by ethanolic extract of Suaeda fruticosa Forssk. Ex J.F.Gmel in animal models of ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology -

item.title: STING activation promotes intestinal mucin secretion in IBD.
item.description: Biochimica et biophysica acta. Molecular basis of disease - Goblet cells produce mucus protecting the intestinal epithelium. Depleted goblet cells and a disrupted mucus layer are observed in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). Remarkably, both deficiency and...

item.title: Autotaxin-Scavenging Nanoliposomes for Prolonged Colon Retention and Autophagy-Mediated Mucosal Immune Restoration in Colitis.
item.description: Biomaterials research - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is an immune-mediated disorder driven by overactivation of autotaxin (ATX), which elevates lysophosphatidic acid (LPA) signaling and suppresses autophagy, exacerbat...

item.title: Silicon-based agent mitigates fatty liver formation in a CDAHFD60-induced MASH mouse model by enhancing hepatic function.
item.description: Biochemistry and biophysics reports - Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) is a progressive liver disorder marked by hepatic inflammation and injury caused by excessive fat accumulation unrelated to alcohol consumpti...

item.title: Fruit and Vegetable Supplemented-Diet Ameliorates Dextran Sodium Sulfate (DSS)-Induced Colitis by Modulating Host Transcriptome and Gut Metagenome Response.
item.description: Nutrients - Background/Objectives: Dietary intake of fruits and vegetables (FVs) has been inversely associated with a lower risk of ulcerative colitis. Using a pig model, we evaluated the effect of FV suppleme...

item.title: A comparative study of dextran sodium sulfate-induced and spleen-kidney syndrome-based ulcerative colitis models.
item.description: Biochemistry and biophysics reports - Accurate animal models are essential for elucidating the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC). The widely used dextran sodium sulfate-induced UC (DSS-UC) model inadequately reflects the refracto...

item.title: UC-associated autoantibodies to αvβ6 inhibit mucosal TGFβ activation and predispose to intestinal inflammation.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is characterized by epithelial barrier dysfunction and dysregulated mucosal immune responses; however, the mechanisms driving disease onset remain poorly defined. Autoantibo...

item.title: Huanglian muxiang decoction alleviates FTH-mediated ferroptosis in colitis by inhibiting the PI3K-AKT pathway.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Dysregulated iron metabolism and subsequent ferroptosis play a critical role in the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC), highlighting iron homeostasis as a therapeutic target. Huanglian muxiang...

item.title: Therapeutic potential of Qihuang Biwen Formula and its bioactive compounds for the treatment of ulcerative colitis in Drosophila and mice.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Qihuang Biwen Formula (QHBWF), derived from the classic Yupingfeng Powder, is traditionally used for immune regulation. While clinically applied, its mechanism of action in ulcerative colitis (UC) ...

item.title: Chemical Profiling and Mechanistic Insights into Stichopodidae Viscus Extract for Ulcerative Colitis via UPLC-IMS-Q-TOF-HDMSE and Network Pharmacology.
item.description: Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland) - Background: The visceral organs of sea cucumbers belonging to the family Stichopodidae, also known as Stichopodidae Viscus (SV), have been traditionally used for the management of gastrointestinal ...

item.title: Xu Chunfu's Modified Xianglian Pill Regulates the NOX2/ROS/Mitochondria/NLRP3 Axis to Treat Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland) - Background/Objectives: Xu Chunfu's Modified Xianglian Pill (XXLP) has been used for centuries in Chinese medicine to treat "diarrhea" and "dysentery," conditions analogous to modern ulcerative coli...

item.title: Purified Diets Lacking Fermentable Fiber Reduce Microbial Diversity, Alter Epithelial Transcriptome, and Exacerbate Colitis.
item.description: Nutrients - Background/Objectives: Dietary fibers play key roles in shaping gut microbiome and intestinal homeostasis. While purified diets offer experimental precision and reproducibility in rodent models, th...

item.title: CU104, a novel barrier function enhancer, improves colitis via modulation of barrier function and immune cell recruitment.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is a chronic and relapsing condition with complex pathogenesis and limited therapeutic options. The efficacy of C...
